Ark.AgentsDetectionEmpty: No risky skills right now. Run Security scan on the Overview if you edited files.
Ark.AgentsDetectionNoCache: No security scan data yet. Run Security scan on the Overview.
Ark.AgentsDetectionNoFindingDetail: No per-finding text (old cache). Run Security scan on the Overview to refresh.
Ark.AgentsDetectionFilterOkEmpty: This page lists only high, medium, and low risk skills. Healthy items are not shown here; see the count on the Overview.
Ark.AgentsDetectionFilterTierEmpty: No skills at this risk level. Check counts on the Overview.
Ark.SkillFindGitleaksProblemGeneric: A gitleaks rule matched patterns that may indicate secrets or policy issues.
Ark.SkillFindGitleaksFix: Edit SKILL.md per the rule description; remove or redact secrets—never commit real keys or tokens.
Ark.SkillFindGitleaksFallback: If unsure: do not sync this skill to untrusted devices; do not publish the folder; try in isolation; refresh rules and rescan.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinSecretsProblem: The text resembles passwords, API keys, private keys, or bearer tokens.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinSecretsFix: Remove real secrets; use placeholders or document env vars; point to a private secret store.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinSecretsFallback: If it must stay as an example: limit Ark Sync to trusted peers; revoke any exposed credentials at the provider.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinDangerousExecProblem: The text mentions eval, dynamic execution, child_process exec/spawn, or risky disk commands.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinDangerousExecFix: Reword to avoid driving untrusted execution; if commands are needed, require human review and least privilege.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinDangerousExecFallbac: If you rely on this: use an isolated environment; do not grant unsupervised system powers; audit changes.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinNetworkProblem: The text references outbound HTTP, external URLs, or WebSockets.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinNetworkFix: State allowed hosts; keep sensitive data local; use fake domains in examples.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinNetworkFallback: If networking is required: run in a monitored setup; avoid pairing with highly confidential data; verify proxy/DNS trust.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinFilesystemProblem: The text references writing/deleting files or invoking shells (PowerShell, bash, etc.).
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinFilesystemFix: Prefer descriptive steps over destructive one-liners; mention backups and confirmation when unavoidable.
Ark.SkillFindBuiltinFilesystemFallback: If you cannot soften it: avoid automation on critical data; keep backups; narrow sync scope.
Ark.AgentsBrowserOnly: Browser preview cannot read local folders. Open this page in the Ark Sync desktop app.
Ark.AgentsEmptySkills: No skills folder or it is empty.
Ark.AgentsNoneDetected: No agents detected on this machine. Run “Environment scan” on the Overview page, or install a supported product and refresh.
Ark.AgentsSkillRiskFilterEmpty: No skills match this risk level. Run “Security scan” on the Overview or change the filter.
Ark.AgentsSkillRiskFallbackAll: No skills match this filter. Showing all skills below; badges reflect scan results (“Not scanned” until you run Security scan on the Overview).
Ark.AgentsSkillRiskHint: Risk labels come from Overview → Security scan (not the environment scan). Without it you’ll see “Run Security scan”; if a folder has no SKILL.md you’ll see “No SKILL.md”.
Ark.DeviceIdError: Could not read this device's ID.
Incorrect configuration may damage your: Incorrect configuration may damage your folder contents and render Ark Sync inoperable.
Ark.SummaryBrowserHintPrefix: Browser preview cannot read local environment paths. Open
Ark.SummaryBrowserHintSuffix: in the desktop app to run scans.
Ark.LanguageNotReadyTip: This language is not fully localized yet
Ark.SummaryOneClickInstallDesktopOnly: One-click install is only available in the Ark Sync desktop app.
Ark.SummaryInstallRunning: Running install script for {{productName}}. Please wait…
Ark.SummaryInstallInvokeFailed: Could not start install flow. Please try again later.
Ark.SummaryInstallNeedManualRescan: Install script finished, but scan result could not refresh. Please run Agent detection manually later.
Ark.SummaryInstallFinishedButNotDetected: Install command finished, but tool is still not detected after refresh. Check logs and PATH/install directory, then scan again.
Ark.SummaryHudEnvAndSkillReady: Environment and SKILL results are ready
Ark.SummaryRiskFilterOkHint: Healthy items are not listed in detail page; click for hint
Ark.FoldersWatcherTip: Regular scanning with enabled file watcher
Ark.FoldersDesktopOnly: This feature is only available in Ark Sync desktop app.
Ark.FoldersLocalScanMode: Relay dir not found, local scan only
Ark.FoldersChanges: Changes: {{copiedToLocal}} to local, {{copiedToRelay}} to relay, {{conflicts}} conflicts, {{skipped}} skipped
Ark.FoldersSyncTip: Check ~/.sync_tmp first, then execute bidirectional sync per mapping rules
Ark.ConnectTagline: AI Agent security and config sync, start from your local device with one click
Ark.ConnectFeatureSkillSecurityDesc: Scan local skills and related files with rule-based detection, flag sensitive info and high-risk operations for self-check before sync.
Ark.ConnectFeatureMigration: Auto-migrate SKILL, Memory, Files across devices
Ark.ConnectFeatureMigrationDesc: Use Ark Sync app to scan multiple devices, automatically sync SKILL, Memory, and Files (config files).
Ark.ConnectErrorBrowserOnly: The "Enter system" feature uses local no-API-key connection and only supports the desktop client. Please use Ark Sync installer or run npm run dev to open an Electron window; for browser mode, use the advanced connection method with an API key.
Ark.SettingsCandidateAlwaysOn: Release candidates always have usage report enabled.
Ark.SettingsCandidateAutoUpgrade: Release candidates always have auto upgrade enabled.
Ark.SettingsGuiAddressOverridden: GUI listen address has been overridden by startup args, changes here may not take effect while override is active.
Ark.SettingsClearPassword: Leave empty and save to clear password; fill in for new password
Ark.SettingsUseHttps: Use HTTPS to connect to GUI
Ark.FoldersEmptyHint: No folders yet. Click "Add folder" to create one.
Ark.AboutDescription: Ark Sync is a desktop and web client for connecting and managing file sync instances. The underlying sync capability is provided by a compatible engine; this app is a independently released client interface.
Ark.AboutFeatures: Key features: Auto-migrate SKILL, Memory, and Files across devices · SKILL security scan to detect sensitive info and high-risk operations · One-click install of third-party AI agents · Multi-device sync via relay directory · Real-time folder status monitoring
Ark.FolderTypeEncryptOnly: Can only set folder type "Receive encrypted" when adding a new folder
Ark.FolderPauseSync: Pause sync when limit is reached; unit can be % or absolute
Ark.FolderVersionPathHelp: Maximum days to keep versions, 0 = keep forever.
Ark.FolderVersionFolderHelp: Path where versions are stored (leave empty to store in .stversions in the share folder).
Ark.FolderCleanIntervalHelp: Interval to run cleaning in version directory (seconds). 0 = disable periodic cleaning.
Ark.RateLimitHelp: Rate limit applies to cumulative traffic of all connections to this device.
Ark.RateLimitNonNegative: Rate limit must be non-negative (0 = unlimited).
Ark.AutoAcceptIn: will auto-accept and save in {cd} seconds...
Ark.AutoAcceptFolderIn: will auto-accept folder and save in {folderCd} seconds...
Ark.ManualEncryptConfig: Please manually complete receiving encrypted config on "Folders" page.
Ark.AddDeviceFromRelay: Add devices from relay to our device list for mutual shared folders.
Ark.AutoShareDefaultPath: Auto-create or share folders this device shows on default path.
Ark.IgnorePatternHelp: Enter ignore patterns, one per line.
Ark.PatternQuickGuide: Quick guide to supported patterns (full docs):
Ark.FolderLabelHelp: Optional descriptive label for the folder. May be different on each device.
Ark.FolderGroupHelp: Optional grouping for folders. Each device can set different groups.
Ark.FolderIdHelp: Identifier required for the folder. Must be the same on all cluster devices.
Ark.FolderPathHelp: Path to the folder on the local machine. Will be created if it doesn't exist. Tilde (~) can be used as shorthand for {hintPath}.
Ark.VersionHelp: Data exists only when folder has versioning enabled. Restoring will recover selected historical version in the folder.
Ark.IgnoreHelp: One pattern per line, same syntax as .stignore. Takes effect immediately after saving.
Ark.SettingsOctalHint: Up to three octal digits (matches official setting).
Ark.SettingsMinDiskSpaceTip: This setting controls the required free space on the main disk (the index database).
Ark.FolderOwnershipSyncDesc: Enable sending ownership information to other devices and apply incoming ownership. Usually requires elevated privileges.
Ark.FolderOwnershipSendDesc: Enable sending ownership information to other devices but not apply incoming ownership. This may have significant performance impact. Enabled automatically when "Sync ownership" is enabled.
Ark.FoldersBlockIndexDesc: Maintain an index of all blocks in the folder so that changed files can be synchronized by reusing blocks from other files. Disabling reduces database size at the cost of not being able to reuse blocks across files.
Ark.FolderIgnorePermsDesc: Disable comparing and syncing file permissions. Useful for systems that don't have or have custom permissions (e.g. FAT, exFAT, Ark Sync, Android).
Ark.FolderXattrSyncDesc: Enable sending extended attributes to other devices and apply incoming extended attributes. May require elevated privileges.
Ark.FolderXattrSendDesc: Enable sending extended attributes to other devices but not apply incoming extended attributes. This may have significant performance impact. Enabled automatically when "Sync extended attributes" is enabled.
